metadata.summary: IHEPDIRAC is the IHEP extension of DIRAC

modules.name: IHEPDIRAC.ResourceStatusSystem.PolicySystem.Actions.EmailAction

EmailAction

This action writes all the necessary data to a cache file ( cache.db ) that
will be used later by the EmailAgent in order to send the emails for each site.

CacheFeederAgent

This agent feeds the Cache tables with the outputs of the cache commands.

The CacheFeederAgent feeds the cache tables for the client and the accounting.
It runs periodically a set of commands, and stores it's results on the
tables.

modules.name: IHEPDIRAC.ResourceStatusSystem.Agent.SAMTestAgent
documentation:
SAMTestAgent

This agent executes SAM tests and evaluates resources and sites SAM status.

classes.name: IHEPDIRAC.ResourceStatusSystem.Agent.SAMTestAgent.SAMTestAgent

methods.name: IHEPDIRAC.ResourceStatusSystem.Agent.SAMTestAgent.SAMTestAgent.initialize
documentation: specify the tests which need to be executed.

methods.name: IHEPDIRAC.ResourceStatusSystem.Agent.SAMTestAgent.SAMTestAgent.execute
documentation:
The main method of the agent. It get elements which need to be tested and
evaluated from CS. Then it instantiates TestExecutor and StatusEvaluate and
calls their main method to finish all the work.

SAMTestAgent

The SAMTestAgent is used to execute SAM tests and evaluate SAM status
periodically. It executes tests with TestExecutor and evaluates status with
StatusEvaluator.

modules.name: IHEPDIRAC.ResourceStatusSystem.SAM.StatusEvaluator
documentation:
StatusEvaluator

StatusEvaluator is used to evaluate elements' SAM status depending on the
SAM tests results. The SAM status includes resource SAM status and site SAM
status. Firstly, integrate tests results and get resource SAM status. Then judge
site SAM status depending on the status of resources which belong to the site.

classes.name: IHEPDIRAC.ResourceStatusSystem.SAM.StatusEvaluator.StatusEvaluator

methods.name: IHEPDIRAC.ResourceStatusSystem.SAM.StatusEvaluator.StatusEvaluator.__init__
documentation:
Constructor

examples:
  >>> sites = { 'CLUSTER' : [ 'CLUSTER.USTC.cn' ],
                          'GRID' : [ 'GRID.JINR.ru' ],
                          'CLOUD' : [ ''CLOUD.IHEP-OPENSTACK.cn' ] }
  >>> evaluator = StatusEvaluator( sites )

:Parameters:
   **sites** - `dict`
     the sites to evaluate SAM status. The sites is grouped by domain.
StatusEvaluator

methods.name: IHEPDIRAC.ResourceStatusSystem.DB.ResourceManagementIHEPDB.ResourceManagementIHEPDB.insert
documentation:
Inserts args in the DB making use of kwargs where parameters such as
the 'table' are specified ( filled automatically by the Client). Typically you
will not pass kwargs to this function, unless you know what are you doing
and you have a very special use case.

```
Parameters

params - 

arguments for the mysql query ( must match table columns ! ).

meta - 

metadata for the mysql query. It must contain, at least,  key
with the proper table name.

return

S_OK() || S_ERROR()
```

methods.name: IHEPDIRAC.ResourceStatusSystem.DB.ResourceManagementIHEPDB.ResourceManagementIHEPDB.update
documentation:
Updates row with values given on args. The row selection is done using the
default of MySQLMonkey ( column.primary or column.keyColumn ). It can be
modified using kwargs. The 'table' keyword argument is mandatory, and
filled automatically by the Client. Typically you will not pass kwargs to
this function, unless you know what are you doing and you have a very
special use case.

```
Parameters

params - 

arguments for the mysql query ( must match table columns ! ).

meta - 

metadata for the mysql query. It must contain, at least,  key
with the proper table name.

return

S_OK() || S_ERROR()
```

methods.name: IHEPDIRAC.ResourceStatusSystem.DB.ResourceManagementIHEPDB.ResourceManagementIHEPDB.select
documentation:
Uses arguments to build conditional SQL statement ( WHERE ... ). If the
sql statement desired is more complex, you can use kwargs to interact with
the MySQL buildCondition parser and generate a more sophisticated query.

```
Parameters

params - 

arguments for the mysql query ( must match table columns ! ).

meta - 

metadata for the mysql query. It must contain, at least,  key
with the proper table name.

return

S_OK() || S_ERROR()
```

methods.name: IHEPDIRAC.ResourceStatusSystem.DB.ResourceManagementIHEPDB.ResourceManagementIHEPDB.delete
documentation:
Uses arguments to build conditional SQL statement ( WHERE ... ). If the
sql statement desired is more complex, you can use kwargs to interact with
the MySQL buildCondition parser and generate a more sophisticated query.
There is only one forbidden query, with all parameters None ( this would
mean a query of the type DELETE * from TableName ). The usage of kwargs
is the same as in the get function.

```
Parameters

params - 

arguments for the mysql query ( must match table columns ! ).

meta - 

metadata for the mysql query. It must contain, at least,  key
with the proper table name.

return

S_OK() || S_ERROR()
```

modules.name: IHEPDIRAC.ResourceStatusSystem.SAM.TestExecutor
documentation:
TestExecutor

TestExecutor is the end-point to executor SAM tests. It loads the tests which
need to be executed dynamically and executes all the tests. At last, it stores
the test results to database.

classes.name: IHEPDIRAC.ResourceStatusSystem.SAM.TestExecutor.TestExecutor

methods.name: IHEPDIRAC.ResourceStatusSystem.SAM.TestExecutor.TestExecutor.__init__
documentation:
Constructor

examples:
  >>> tests = { 'WMS-Test' : { 'module' : 'WMSTest',
                                                       'args' : { 'executable' : [ '/usr/bin/python',
                                                                       'wms_test.py' ], 'timeout' : 1800 } } }
  >>> elements = { 'ComputingElement' : [ 'chenj01.ihep.ac.cn' ],
                                   'StorageElement', [ 'IHEPD-USER' ],
                                   'CLOUD' : [ 'CLOUD.IHEP-OPENSTACK.cn' ] }
  >>> executor = TestExecutor( tests, elements )
  >>> executor1 = TestExecutor( tests, elements, { 'ResourceManagementClient' :
                                                                                                 ResourceManagementClient() } )

:Parameters:
  **tests** - `dict`
    dictionary with tests to be executed. The test class is loaded according to the
    'module' key and instantiated with 'args' key.
  **elements** - `dict`
    the elements need to be tested. The elements is grouped by type.
  **apis** - 'dict'
    dictionary with clients to be used in the commands issued by the policies.
    If not defined, the commands will import them.

methods.name: IHEPDIRAC.ResourceStatusSystem.SAM.TestExecutor.TestExecutor.__storeTestResults
documentation: store the test results.

methods.name: IHEPDIRAC.ResourceStatusSystem.SAM.TestExecutor.TestExecutor.execute
documentation:
Main method which executes the tests and obtains the results. Use
two loops to do all the work. In the first loop, execute all the tests for
corresponding elements and put the executed tests into executedTestsQueue.
In the second loop, traverse executedTestsQueue to obtain test results.

examples:
: ```pycon
  >>> executor.execute()[ 'Value' ]
      { 'Records' : ( ( 'chenj01.ihep.ac.cn', 'WMS-Test', 'ComputingElement', 'OK',
                                   'balabala', 1, '2016-5-8 00:00:00', '2016-5-8 00:05:23', 0.1234 ),
                                ( 'chenj01.ihep.ac.cn', 'BOSS-Test', 'ComputingElement', 'Bad',
                                   'balabala', 2, '2016-5-8 00:00:00', '0000-0-0', 0 ),
                                ( 'IHEPD-USER', 'SE-Test', 'StorageElement', 'Bad',
                                  'balabala', None, '2016-5-8 00:00:00', '0000-0-0', 0 ) ),
         'Columns' : ( 'ElementName', 'TestType', 'ElementType', 'Status',
                                  'Log', 'JobID', 'SubmissionTime', 'CompletionTime', 'ApplicationTime' ) }
  ```

```
return

S_OK( { 'Records' : , 'Columns' :  } ) / S_ERROR
```
TestExecutor

The ResourceManagementHandler exposes the DB front-end functions through a
XML-RPC server, functionalities inherited from {class}`DIRAC.Core.DISET.Reques  tHandler.RequestHandler`

According to the ResourceManagementDB philosophy, only functions of the type:
\- insert
\- update
\- select
\- delete

are exposed. If you need anything more complicated, either look for it on the
{class}`ResourceManagementClient`, or code it yourself. This way the DB and the
Service are kept clean and tidied.

To can use this service on this way, but you MUST NOT DO IT. Use it through the
{class}`ResourceManagementClient`. If offers in the worst case as good perfor mance as the {class}`ResourceManagementHandler`, if not better.

> ```pycon
> >>> from DIRAC.Core.DISET.RPCClient import RPCCLient
> >>> server = RPCCLient("ResourceStatus/ResourceManagement")
> ```

modules.name: IHEPDIRAC.TransformationSystem.scripts.ihepdirac_transformation_transfer_dir
documentation:
Start transfer of all files under directory with transformation system
Usage:
 ihepdirac-transformation-transfer-dir [option|cfgfile] TransferName DFCDir SourceSE DestSE

Example: 
 $ihepdirac-transformation-transfer-dir transfer_jinr_ihep_2022 /juno/production/muon/prd010 IHEP-JUNOEOS JINR-EOS

Note:
 Only suitable for one level of directories.
!/usr/bin/env python

modules.name: IHEPDIRAC.ResourceStatusSystem.Client.ResourceManagementIHEPClient
documentation:
ResourceManagementIHEPClient

ResourceManagementIHEPClient for IHEPDIRAC.

methods.name: IHEPDIRAC.ResourceStatusSystem.Client.ResourceManagementIHEPClient.ResourceManagementIHEPClient.__init__
documentation:
The client tries to connect to :class:ResourceManagementIHEPDB by default. If it
fails, then tries to connect to the Service :class:ResourceManagementIHEPHandler.

methods.name: IHEPDIRAC.ResourceStatusSystem.Client.ResourceManagementIHEPClient.ResourceManagementIHEPClient.selectJobCache
documentation:
Gets from JobCache all rows that match the parameters given.

:Parameters:
  **site** - `[, string, list ]`
    name of the site element
  **maskStatus** - `[, string, list ]`
    maskStatus for the site
  **efficiency** - `[, float, list ]`
    job efficiency ( successful / total )
  **running** - `[, integer, list ]`
    number of running jobs
  **waiting** - `[, integer, list ]`
    number of waiting jobs
  **done** - `[, integer, list ]`
    number of done jobs
  **failed** - `[, integer, list ]`
    number of failed jobs
  **completed** - `[, integer, list ]`
    number of completed jobs
  **stalled** - `[, integer, list ]`
    number of stalled jobs
  **status** - `[, string, list ]`
    status for the site computed
  **lastCheckTime** - `[, datetime, list ]`
    time-stamp setting last time the result was checked
  **meta** - `[, dict ]`
    meta-data for the MySQL query. It will be filled automatically with the       `table` key and the proper table name.

methods.name: IHEPDIRAC.ResourceStatusSystem.Client.ResourceManagementIHEPClient.ResourceManagementIHEPClient._query
documentation: It is a simple helper, this way inheriting classes can use it.

methods.name: IHEPDIRAC.ResourceStatusSystem.Client.ResourceManagementIHEPClient.ResourceManagementIHEPClient.__query
documentation:
This method is a rather important one. It will format the input for the DB
queries, instead of doing it on a decorator. Two dictionaries must be passed
to the DB. First one contains 'columnName' : value pairs, being the key
lower camel case. The second one must have, at lease, a key named 'table'
with the right table name.

'SEStatus' : 'Bad'

types_getSitesSAMSummary = []

methods.name: IHEPDIRAC.ResourceStatusSystem.Service.PublisherIHEPHandler.PublisherIHEPHandler.export_getSitesStorageSummary

return

'FreeStorage' :  200.00
'StorageUsage' : 80.0
} } ) | S_ERROR
```

methods.name: IHEPDIRAC.ResourceStatusSystem.Service.PublisherIHEPHandler.PublisherIHEPHandler.export_getSitesJobSummary
documentation:
Return the dictionary with job summary information for the given sites.

```
return

S_OK( { site : { 'Running' :  222
'Waiting' :  222
'Done' : 222
'Failed' : 222
'Efficiency' : 50.0
'MaxJobs' : 666
'JobUsage' : 33.3
} } ) | S_ERROR
```

} } ) | S_ERROR
```

methods.name: IHEPDIRAC.ResourceStatusSystem.Service.PublisherIHEPHandler.PublisherIHEPHandler.export_getSiteWNsInfo
documentation:
Retruns the jobs statistics for hosts of the given site.

```
return

S_OK( [ { 'Host' : 'aaa.bb.ccc'
'Running' : 1
'Done' : 22
'Failed' : 22
'Efficiency' : 50.0
} ] ) / S_ERROR
```

methods.name: IHEPDIRAC.ResourceStatusSystem.Service.PublisherIHEPHandler.PublisherIHEPHandler.export_getSAMDetail
documentation: Returns the dictionary with SAM test detail information for the given test.

methods.name: IHEPDIRAC.ResourceStatusSystem.Service.PublisherIHEPHandler.PublisherIHEPHandler.export_getSAMSummary
documentation:
Returns SAM tests status for the elements of the given site.

```
return

S_OK( { element : { 'ElementType' :
'WMSTest' :
'CVMFSTest' :
'BOSSTest' :
'SETest' : } } ) / S_ERROR
```
PublisherHandler inherits from DIRAC's PublisherHandler.

modules.name: IHEPDIRAC.TransformationSystem.scripts.ihepdirac_transformation_rmfiles
documentation: Create a production to remove files from all storage elements
!/bin/env python

functions.name: IHEPDIRAC.TransformationSystem.scripts.ihepdirac_transformation_rmfiles.main
documentation: reads command line parameters, makes check and creates transformation

modules.name: IHEPDIRAC.TransformationSystem.scripts.ihepdirac_transformation_reset_files
documentation:
Reset files in transformation with specified status

Usage:
 ihepdirac-transformation-reset-files [option|cfgfile] TransID1 [TransID2 ...]

Example: 
 $ihepdirac-transformation-reset-files -t Problematic,Assigned 123 456
!/usr/bin/env python

modules.name: IHEPDIRAC.TransformationSystem.scripts.ihepdirac_transformation_transfer_metadata
documentation:
Start transfer according to DFC query with transformation system

Usage:
  ihepdirac-transformation-transfer-metadata [option|cfgfile] TransformationName MetaTransfer TargetSE

Example:
  $ihepdirac-transformation-transfer-metadata Meassurements_DAQ_CNAF juno_transfer=Pmt/container/Meassurements CNAF-STORM
  $ihepdirac-transformation-transfer-metadata Meassurements_DAQ_IN2P3 -p Standard juno_transfer=Pmt/container/Meassurements IN2P3-DCACHE
  $ihepdirac-transformation-transfer-metadata Meassurements_DAQ_JINR -t Transfer-JUNO juno_transfer=Pmt/container/Meassurements -f IHEP-STORM JINR-JUNO
!/usr/bin/env python

modules.name: IHEPDIRAC.TransformationSystem.scripts.ihepdirac_juno_make_reconstruction_sim
documentation:
Create JUNO reconstruction with detsim data

Usage:
 ihepdirac-juno-make-reconstruction-sim [option|cfgfile] [process]

Example: 
 $ihepdirac-juno-make-reconstruction-sim --example > rec.ini
 $ihepdirac-juno-make-reconstruction-sim --ini rec.ini
 $ihepdirac-juno-make-reconstruction-sim Chain
 $ihepdirac-juno-make-reconstruction-sim --ini rec.ini --dryrun
!/usr/bin/env python

functions.name: IHEPDIRAC.TransformationSystem.scripts.ihepdirac_juno_make_reconstruction_sim._checkProxy
documentation: checks if the proxy has the ProductionManagement property and belongs to a VO

methods.name: IHEPDIRAC.WorkloadManagementSystem.Agent.TaskAgent.TaskAgent.execute
documentation: Main execution method

methods.name: IHEPDIRAC.WorkloadManagementSystem.Agent.TaskAgent.TaskAgent.__refreshTaskStatus
documentation: Refresh the task status

methods.name: IHEPDIRAC.WorkloadManagementSystem.Agent.TaskAgent.TaskAgent.__getTaskAttribute
documentation: Get all attributes of the jobs in the task

methods.name: IHEPDIRAC.WorkloadManagementSystem.Agent.TaskAgent.TaskAgent.__refreshTaskStringAttribute
documentation: Refresh the task attribute. The attribute type must be string and seperated by comma

The specific agents must provide the following methods:
- initialize() for initial settings
- beginExecution()
- execute() - the main method called in the agent cycle
- endExecution()
- finalize() - the graceful exit of the method, this one is usually used
           for the agent restart

modules.name: IHEPDIRAC.TransformationSystem.scripts.ihepdirac_juno_make_reconstruction

modules.name: IHEPDIRAC.DataManagementSystem.scripts.ihepdirac_dms_add_dir
documentation:
Add all the files under specified directory to SE and DFC

Usage:
  ihepdirac_dms_add_dir [option|cfgfile] DFCDir LocalDir SE

Example:
  ihepdirac_dms_add_dir /juno/user/z/zhangxm/9743 /junofs/grid/user/z/zhangxm/9743 IHEP-JUNOEOS
! /usr/bin/env python

modules.name: IHEPDIRAC.DataManagementSystem.scripts.ihepdirac_dms_register_dir
documentation:
Register SE files under specified directory to DFC. Files must be locally readable

Usage:
  ihepdirac_dms_register_dir [option|cfgfile] DFCDir LocalDir SE

Example:
  ihepdirac_dms_register_dir /juno/user/z/zhangxm/9743 /junofs/grid/user/z/zhangxm/9743 IHEP-JUNOEOS
! /usr/bin/env python

modules.name: IHEPDIRAC.DataManagementSystem.scripts.ihepdirac_dms_register_files
documentation:
Register SE files from a list of files to DFC. These list of files must be locally readable

Usage:
  ihepdirac_dms_register_files [option|cfgfile] DFCRoot LocalRoot Filelist SE

Example:
  $ ihepdirac_dms_register_files /juno/lustre/junofs/PmtCharacterization/scan_data/soft/root_macros /junofs/PmtCharacterization/scan_data/soft/root_macros filelist.txt IHEP-STORM
! /usr/bin/env python

modules.name: IHEPDIRAC.DataManagementSystem.scripts.ihepdirac_dms_register_EOS_files
documentation:
Register files in EOS SE Directory to DFC.

Usage:
  ihepdirac_dms_register_EOS_files [option|cfgfile] DFCDir EosDir SE 

Example:
  $ ihepdirac_dms_register_EOS_files /juno/raw/test_register root://junoeos01.ihep.ac.cn:1094//eos/juno/dirac/juno/raw/test_register IHEP-JUNOEOS
!/usr/bin/env python

modules.name: IHEPDIRAC.DataManagementSystem.scripts.ihepdirac_dms_check_replicas
documentation:
Check if all files under specified DFC directory have a replica in specified SEs (splitted by commas).

Usage:
  ihepdirac_dms_check_replicas [option|cfgfile] DFCDir SEs

Example:
  ihepdirac_dms_check_replicas /juno/user/z/zhangxm/9743 IHEP-JUNOEOS
! /usr/bin/env python
